ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for kaya bun; mapped to broader bread product class. Kaya bun is a filled sweet bread roll specific to Singaporean cuisine.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for kaya bun; A000E (Bread and similar products) is the closest defensible broader category anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for kaya bun as a specific food concept.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard kaya bun weighs approximately 80 g and is typically consumed as a single breakfast or snack serving.

nutrient_source.title: Kaya Bun — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Kaya Bun (cr_id F05020058). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are taken from this canonical entry with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: White bread and sweet filled bread rolls — GI analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for white bread (GI 70–75) and sweet buns
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for kaya bun in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 70 is estimated by analogy to white bread (GI 70, University of Sydney GI Database) and sweet enriched bread rolls, which share a refined wheat flour base and similar sugar content. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (70) × available carbohydrate per serving (55.6 g × 0.80 kg serving fraction) / 100 = 70 × 44.48 / 100 ≈ 31.1. This is a conservative analogous estimate; actual GI may be higher due to the concentrated kaya sugar filling.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (18.4 g/100g) with low dietary fibre (1.5 g/100g) drives insulin-mediated sodium retention; sodium is 168 mg/100g
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit kaya bun intake to occasional consumption, as the high sugar load (18.4 g/100g) promotes insulin-mediated sodium retention even though absolute sodium (168 mg/100g) is moderate; choosing wholemeal variants with higher fibre would reduce this effect.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate (55.6 g/100g), high sugar (18.4 g/100g exceeding 15 g/100g threshold), low dietary fibre (1.5 g/100g), and estimated high GI of 70 combine to produce a high glycemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: Kaya bun is not recommended for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its high sugar content (18.4 g/100g), high refined carbohydrate load (55.6 g/100g), and estimated GI of 70, which together produce a glycemic load of approximately 31 per serving and are expected to cause significant postprandial glucose elevation; if consumed, portion should be limited to half a bun and paired with a protein source such as a boiled egg.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~2.8 g/100g), trans fat (0.1 g/100g from margarine), and high sugar (18.4 g/100g) contribute to atherogenic and inflammatory risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume kaya bun sparingly, as the combination of saturated fat, trace trans fat from margarine, and high sugar content (18.4 g/100g) contributes to dyslipidaemia and endothelial inflammation; butter-based kaya variants without trans fat are preferable if consumed occasionally.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Sugar at 18.4 g/100g exceeds the 15 g/100g RA threshold; refined wheat flour with minimal fibre (1.5 g/100g) drives high glycaemic load; trace trans fat (0.1 g/100g) is pro-inflammatory
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Kaya bun should be avoided during rheumatoid arthritis flares, as the high sugar content (18.4 g/100g), refined carbohydrate base, and trace trans fat from margarine collectively amplify systemic inflammation; butter-based variants with lower trans fat are preferable if occasional consumption is desired.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Kaya bun (咖椰包; Roti kaya) is a popular Singaporean bakery item consisting of a soft, lightly sweetened white bread roll filled with kaya — a rich, fragrant coconut jam made from coconut milk, eggs, sugar, and pandan leaf. The bun has a pillowy, slightly chewy texture with a sweet, aromatic filling that carries distinct pandan and coconut flavours. It is a staple breakfast and snack food widely available at kopitiams (traditional coffee shops), bakeries, and hawker centres across Singapore. Per 100 g, kaya bun provides 316 kcal, 7.2 g protein, 7.2 g fat, 55.6 g carbohydrate, 18.4 g sugar, 1.5 g dietary fibre, and 168 mg sodium. The high sugar content (18.4 g/100g) and refined carbohydrate base with low fibre make it a high-glycaemic food (estimated GI 70) with a glycemic load of approximately 31 per standard 80 g serving. Clinically, kaya bun is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or rheumatoid arthritis due to its high sugar and refined carbohydrate load, and should be consumed only occasionally by those managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The kaya filling may contain trace trans fat from margarine in commercial preparations.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a kaya bun?
Is kaya bun suitable for hypertension?
What is the GI of kaya bun?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F05020058) with confidence 0.95. This is the canonical primary source for Singaporean food composition. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using white bread as the reference food, which is a defensible and documented estimation.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for kaya bun is not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry (F05020058) as of April 2026. potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrients and moisture data are not reported in the HPB Foodo entry for this food item. trans_fat_g set to 0.1 based on known use of margarine in commercial kaya bun production; this is an estimated trace value consistent with HPB guidance on commercial bakery products. saturated_fat_g estimated at 2.8 g/100g based on total fat of 7.2 g/100g and typical saturated fat fraction for enriched bread rolls from USDA SR Legacy (bread, white, commercially prepared, USDA NDB 18069) as a supporting reference for fatty acid distribution. GI of 70 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database white bread reference; no direct kaya bun GI measurement is available in any approved source. Glycemic load per serving calculated as GI (70) × net carbohydrate per 80 g serving (44.48 g) / 100 ≈ 31.1. All core macros (protein_g 7.2, fat_g 7.2, carbohydrate_g 55.6) are non-zero and sourced from HPB Foodo; no zero-macro justification required.
